figures.caption: Fig. 4. The performance comparison of Instruct2Act, Vanilla EA, VIMA, and our proposed method.
TABLE I OVERALL PERFORMANCE COMPARISON OF VIMA-BENCH IN SELECTED TASK GENERALIZATIONS.

Fig. 2. The overview architecture of EEAgent. The environment interpreter, is implemented using a function-calling VLM and predefined tools. It uses the SAM model to extract entities from the environment and a VLM to interpret them. The policy planner, implemented with an LLM, takes the instruction, environmental information, and prompt as input, producing an executable action sequence as output.

Fig. 1. The motivation for introducing EEAgent is inspired by the human ability to learn from past successes and failures, extracting insights that can inform both present and future tasks. Building on this concept, we leverage short-term memory to guide current tasks and long-term memory to inform future tasks, enabling dynamic prompt optimization and fostering the selfevolution of the embodied agent.
